title: A10. Riemann Hypothesis Super-Rigidity Gap
label: mx1_transmute_07
task_text:
Solve this Super-Eywa grading-bank question.
Question ID: atlas-derived-A10-riemann-hypothesis-super-rigidity-gap
Title: A10. Riemann Hypothesis Super-Rigidity Gap

Problem:
Can a Hermitian matrix eigenvalue spectrum match the Riemann zeta zeros' 47% super-rigidity (Delta_3_sat = 0.155)?

Grading target:
Binary:

- correct conclusion
- identification of the quantitative gap: 0.155 vs 0.24

Return format:
FINAL_ANSWER: <your answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>
root_result_excerpt:
Simplified the prompt to focus on the core mathematical comparison and the required grading criteria.
node_root_helper_01 (transmuted_child): Determine if a Hermitian matrix eigenvalue spectrum can match the Riemann zeta zeros' super-rigidity value of Delta_3_sat = 0.155. To receive full credit, you must provide a binary conclusion and identify the quantitative gap between the Riemann zeta zeros (
final_result_excerpt:
FINAL_ANSWER: No, gap is 0.085
JUSTIFICATION: 0.24 minus 0.155 equals 0.085.

root_orchestration.decision_notes: Simplified the prompt to focus on the core mathematical comparison and the required grading criteria.

grading.expected:
No.

Best construction achieves Delta_3_sat = 0.24.
GUE statistics are easy, but super-rigidity requires prime-orbit structure no matrix encodes.
grading.grading_notes: The user correctly identified the binary conclusion (No) and correctly identified the quantitative gap (0.24 - 0.155 = 0.085), satisfying both requirements of the grading target.
